description: A plant embryo with the store or covering that supports its early growth is a 'thinoe'. Plants that reproduce through spores fall outside this category. 'powea' (egg) belongs to animal reproduction, while 'thowia' (born) marks the arrival event.
articulatory_notes: For 'thi', the tongue touches the upper teeth lightly while unvoiced air passes into high-front 'i'. Dental nasal 'n' carries voice into stressed rounded 'o'; the final front 'e' stays distinct across the hiatus.
usage_notes: A viable seed can take optional 'lipha'; one counted as food or stored material can take 'themo'. 'thophe' (sow) is the planting verb.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Saving seed joins one season's harvest to another season's planting. Who may keep and share a variety belongs to community agreement, not to the noun.

semantic_domains.nature: a plant reproductive structure containing an embryo
semantic_domains.temporal: a dormant or developing beginning carried across seasons
